coredata.dc:title: Silver nanosol SERS/Flu/Abs trimode analysis of trace H2O2 based on Fe-doped carbon dot catalytic oxidation of TMB
coredata.dc:creator: Li, Jingjing
Zhao, Yuxiang
Sun, Lin
Liang, Aihui
Jiang, Zhiliang
coredata.dc:description: A stable Fe-doped carbon dot (CDFe) nanosol with high catalytic activity was prepared by water hydrothermal procedure using ferrocene as precursor. In Tris-HCl buffer solution, 3,3′,5,5′-tetramethylbenzidine (TMB) was oxidized slowly by H2O2 that catalyzed quickly by CDFe. The oxidation product (TMBox) molecular probes exhibit surface-enhanced Raman scattering (SERS) peak at 1606 cm−1 in the silver nanosol substrate, fluorescence (Flu) peak at 400 nm and absorption (Abs) peak at 560 nm. With the increase of H2O2 concentration, the produced TMBox increased, and the SERS/Flu/Abs peaks were enhanced linearly. Based on this, a new SERS/Flu/Abs trimode molecular spectral method was established for the determination of trace H2O2 based on CDFe catalytic amplification. The results show that the SERS linear range for H2O2 concentration is 5.0 × 10−10–2.5 × 10−8 mol/L, and the detection limit is 2.52 × 10−10 mol/L. The H2O2 contents in food samples were analyzed by this SERS quantitative method, with relative standard deviations of 2.1–4.3 % and recovery of 94.5−103.8 %.
coredata.dcterms:subject: CDFe catalysis
H2O2
TMB
SERS

xocs:item-toc-entry.xocs:item-toc-section-title: Electron microscopy and X-ray diffraction (XRD) of CDFe
Abs/RRS/fluorescence/ Fourier transform infrared spectrometer (FTIR) spectra of CDFe

xocs:funding.xocs:funding-agency-matched-string: National Natural Science Foundation of China
xocs:funding.xocs:funding-agency: National Natural Science Foundation of China

xocs:funding-list.xocs:funding-text: This work was supported by the National Natural Science Foundation of China (No. 21767004 ).

ce:caption.ce:simple-para: Analytical principle of detecting H2O2 by CDFe catalysis-SERS/Flu/Abs trimode spectrometry.
Electron microscopy, diameter distribution, energy spectrum and XRD of CDFe.
A: TEM and particle size distribution histogram of CDFe, B: energy spectrum of CDFe, C: XRD patterns of CDFe and ferrocene.

A: Abs spectra of CDFe, a∼f curves represent 60, 75, 100, 120, 300, 600 μg/mL CDFe, respectively; B: RRS spectra of CDFe, a∼f curves represent 60, 75, 100, 120, 300, 600 μg/mL CDFe, respectively; C: Fluorescence spectra of CDFe under different excitation wavelengths, 600 μg/mL CDFe; D: Fluorescence spectra of CDFe, a∼f curves represent 6, 12, 60, 120, 300, 600 μg/mL CDFe, respectively; E: Infrared spectra of CDFe and ferrocene.

A: SERS, (0, 0.5, 1.5, 2.5, 3.5, 5, 10, 15, 25) nmol/L H2O2+0.225 μg/mL CDFe+31 μmol/L TMB + 2.5 mmol/L Tris-HCl+0.8 × 10−4 mol/L AgNPs; B: fluorescence, (0, 10, 15, 20, 25, 30, 35, 40, 45) nmol/L H2O2+0.225 μg/mL CDFe+31 μmol/L TMB + 2.5 mmol/L Tris-HCl; C: Abs, (0, 10, 15, 20, 25, 30, 35, 40, 45) nmol/L H2O2+0.225 μg/mL CDFe+31 μmol/L TMB + 2.5 mmol/L Tris-HCl.
SERS determination results of H2O2 in food samples.

head.ce:title: Silver nanosol SERS/Flu/Abs trimode analysis of trace H2O2 based on Fe-doped carbon dot catalytic oxidation of TMB

ce:affiliation.ce:label: a
ce:affiliation.ce:textfn: Key Laboratory of Ecology of Rare and Endangered Species and Environmental Protection (Guangxi Normal University), Ministry of Education, Guilin 541004, China

sa:affiliation.sa:organization: Key Laboratory of Ecology of Rare and Endangered Species and Environmental Protection (Guangxi Normal University)

ce:affiliation.ce:label: b
ce:affiliation.ce:textfn: Guangxi Key Laboratory of Environmental Pollution Control Theory and Technology for Science and Education Combined with Science and Technology Innovation Base, Guilin 541004, China

sa:affiliation.sa:organization: Guangxi Key Laboratory of Environmental Pollution Control Theory and Technology for Science and Education Combined with Science and Technology Innovation Base

ce:affiliation.ce:source-text: Guangxi Key Laboratory of Environmental Pollution Control Theory and Technology for Science and Education Combined with Science and Technology Innovation Base, Guilin 541004, China.

ce:correspondence.ce:text: Corresponding authors at: Key Laboratory of Ecology of Rare and Endangered Species and Environmental Protection (Guangxi Normal University), Ministry of Education, Guilin 541004, China.

sa:affiliation.sa:organization: Key Laboratory of Ecology of Rare and Endangered Species and Environmental Protection (Guangxi Normal University)

ce:abstract-sec.ce:simple-para: A stable Fe-doped carbon dot (CDFe) nanosol with high catalytic activity was prepared by water hydrothermal procedure using ferrocene as precursor. In Tris-HCl buffer solution, 3,3′,5,5′-tetramethylbenzidine (TMB) was oxidized slowly by H2O2 that catalyzed quickly by CDFe. The oxidation product (TMBox) molecular probes exhibit surface-enhanced Raman scattering (SERS) peak at 1606 cm−1 in the silver nanosol substrate, fluorescence (Flu) peak at 400 nm and absorption (Abs) peak at 560 nm. With the increase of H2O2 concentration, the produced TMBox increased, and the SERS/Flu/Abs peaks were enhanced linearly. Based on this, a new SERS/Flu/Abs trimode molecular spectral method was established for the determination of trace H2O2 based on CDFe catalytic amplification. The results show that the SERS linear range for H2O2 concentration is 5.0 × 10−10–2.5 × 10−8 mol/L, and the detection limit is 2.52 × 10−10 mol/L. The H2O2 contents in food samples were analyzed by this SERS quantitative method, with relative standard deviations of 2.1–4.3 % and recovery of 94.5−103.8 %.

ce:section.ce:para: H2O2, as a strong oxidant, has the functions of sterilization, cleaning, drifting etc., and is widely used in food production [1]. In the food industry, H2O2 is mainly used for disinfection of flexible packaging paper, disinfection of cannery, sterilization of milk and dairy products, bread fermentation and decolorization of food fiber. At the same time, it was also used as an auxiliary material in the food industry, as a bleaching agent and antibacterial agent for food such as wheat flour, edible oil, agar, protein, and cheese. Some countries clearly stipulate that in the process of food processing, the use of H2O2 should not exceed the limit. Excessive H2O2 may be left on the surface or inside of the food due to improper use in food factories. Industrial H2O2 contains various kinds of hazardous substances such as heavy metals whose excessive intake will pose a threat to human health. It can reduce the body's antioxidant capacity by depleting the body's antioxidant substances, causing DNA damage and gene mutation in the human body, resulting in various serious diseases, and increasing the risk of cancer [2]. Therefore, the establishment of a method to determine the content of H2O2 is of great significance to food safety inspection. At present, different techniques have been used to detect H2O2, such as colorimetry [3], electrochemical method [4,5], chemiluminescence method [6], fluorescence method and so on [7]. Ping et al. developed an amperometric sensor for the determination of H2O2 in aseptically packaged beverages at 0.1 μM-0.12 mM, with a detection limit of 0.05 μM [8]. Based on the fact that triphenylphosphine can be oxidized to triphenyl oxide by H2O2, Ivanova et al. proposed a high performance liquid chromatography for the determination of H2O2 in milk. The linear range was 2.58 mg/L to 49.88 mg/L, and the detection limit was 0.28 mg/L [9]. Liu et al. developed an Au/Co3O4-CeOx nanocomposite with peroxidase activity as a colorimetric sensor, which effectively oxidized TMB and detected H2O2 of 10−1000 μM, with a detection limit of 5.29 μM [10]. However, in the above methods, some processes are complex, and some have low sensitivity.

Surface-enhanced Raman scattering (SERS) is a powerful molecular spectral technology that combines vibrational spectroscopy and nanotechnology. Raman scattering signal could be increased significantly and extremely low concentrations of analytes could also be detected because of its fingerprint, high sensitivity, and fast detection speed [11]. Since its sensitivity is closely related to the properties of molecular probes and SERS substrates, it is of great necessity to select molecular probes, and prepare SERS substrates with prominent enhancement factors [12,13]. Nanoparticles have become the research object of nanotechnology due to their catalytic activity [14,15]. With the development of technology, various nano-substrates with small size, high SERS activity and excellent stability had been prepared [16,17]. Noble metal nanomaterials have a strong SERS enhancement effect due to their unique surface plasmon resonance (SPR) characteristics and are often used as SERS active matrix [18,19]. SPR properties are determined by the size, shape, particle spacing, arrangement and dielectric environment around the metal nanostructures. Therefore, the key to the wide application of SERS technology is to develop plasmon resonance structure with novel geometry to enhance Raman signals [20,21]. It is reported that the material developed for the catalytic process were also successful in SERS substrate for the detection of probe such as Pt@DNA [22], gold nanoshells [23], IrNP@DNA [24] and RuO2-DNA [25]. At present, SERS spectroscopy has been applied to environmental analysis [26], material characterization [27], food detection [28], medicine [29] and other fields. In order to acquire excellent SERS substrates, Kang synthesized Au@Ag shell-core nanoparticles by using a seed growth method and developed a new SERS biosensor which could be used to detect 0.5−10 ppb of methamphetamine. The detection limit was 0.16 ppb, and the process was simple, quick, and selective [30]. Liu et al. created a new SERS label with double-layer Raman dye marking by adding an integral Ag case on SiO2 core. A rapid and ultra-sensitive detection method for anti-SARS-CoV-2 IgM/IgG in clinical specimens based on SERS was established [31]. Sun et al. found a SERS approach for rapid determination of zirconium in apple and pear extracts by using octyl mercaptan-functionalized core-shell nanoparticles as SERS active substrate. The LODs in the apple and pear samples were detected to be 0.015 and 0.016 ppm, and the LOQs were 0.048 and 0.053 ppm, respectively [32]. Li et al. developed a graphene oxide nanobelt, which could effectively catalyze the reduction of HAuCl4 by H2O2 to generate gold nanoparticles (AuNPs) with SERS activity. It could be combined with Apt reaction to detect Pb (II) quantitatively at 0.002−0.075 μM, with a detection limit of 0.7 nM [33]. As far as we know, there have been no report on the SERS quantitative determination of trace H2O2 based on Fe-doped carbon dots catalytic amplification. Compared with the single-mode method, the multi-mode method not only has the advantages of single-mode, but also has more selectivity. Fan et al. reported a color/fluorescence dual-mode nanosensor for detection of 2,4,6-trinitrophenol and pH, based on green-fluorescent carbon quantum dots [34]. Li et al. fabricated a single-atom Fe catalyst by organic solvent hydrothermal procedure using ferrocene as precursor, and utilized to detect trace pollutants by SERS and resonance Rayleigh scattering (RRS) di-mode method [35]. Wang et al. prepared fullerene carbon dot sol with high catalysis and coupled the nanocatalytic amplification with aptamer reaction to assay of ultratrace As+3 by SERS/RRS/Abs trimode method [36]. To our best knowledge, there are no reports the CDFe nanosol was prepared by water hydrothermal procedure using ferrocene as precursor, and SERS/Flu/Abs trimode method was used to determine H2O2 based on the CDFe catalysis.

Carbon dots (CDs) are a new type of carbon nanomaterials with favorable catalytic functions which are used in analytical chemistry because of their small size, low cost, high stability, low toxicity, and easy surface modification. CDs also have the catalytic activity of peroxidase, and inorganic metal ions can be doped into CDs in order to make them have excellent nanozymes activity [37]. Fe is a common metal element compatible with carbon-based materials. CDFe have strong peroxidase activity and have served in various quantitative analysis. CDFe shows high peroxidase-like activity, stability, and sensitivity, compared with Zn-doped carbon dots (Zn-CDs) [38], zinc-based zeolitic imidazolate framework (ZIF-67) [39], and Zinc-Doped Mesoporous Graphitic Carbon Nitride (Zn-mpg-C3N4) [40]. Lu et al. synthesized CDFe which could oxidize o-phenylenediamine to Ox-OPD. Based on this, a ratiometric fluorescence system was constructed to detect l-Cysteine in the range of 0.25−90 μM, and the detection limit was 0.05 μM [41]. Zhu et al. synthesized fluorescent CDFe with FeCl3·6H2O as the precursor, which catalyzed the oxidation of OPD. And they constructed a ratiometric fluorescence sensing system to detect H2O2 and glucose in the range of 0−133 μM and 0−300 μM, with detection limits of 0.47 μM and 2.5 μM, respectively [42]. Yue et al. constructed a dual emission ratio fluorescence sensing system based on nitrogen and iron co-doped carbon dots (N, Fe-CDs) with peroxidase activity, which detected H2O2 and uric acid (UA) in the range of 0.6−100 μM and 0.8−133 μM, with detection limits of 0.30 μM and 0.49 μM, respectively [43]. Yang et al. synthesized Fe-N-CDs in a solvothermal way, which could effectively catalyze the oxidation of TMB by H2O2. The carcinoembryonic antigen (CEA) in the range of 1–1000 pg/m could be detected photometrically with a detection limit of 0.1 pg/mL [44]. In addition, Zhuo et al. prepared manganese-doped carbon dots (Mn-CDs), which could oxidize TMB to a blue oxidation product (TMBox), and ascorbic acid could reduce TMBox to TMB. Based on this, a colorimetric method was established to determine ascorbic acid in the range of 50–2500 nM, with a detection limit of 9 nM [45]. In this paper, ferrocene was used as a precursor, and CDFe nanosol catalyst was prepared by the water hydrothermal procedure, which catalyzed the oxidation of TMB by H2O2 to generate TMBox molecular probes with SERS activity. The purpose of this paper is to propose a new trimode method for rapid and sensitive determination of H2O2 in waterishlogged food products.

Thermo DXR smart Raman spectrometer (Thermo Scientific Company, USA). A model of F-7100 Hitachi fluorescence spectrometer (Hitachi Company, Tokyo, Japan) was used to scan fluorescence spectra and RRS spectra (at volt = 350 V, excited slit = emission slit = 5 nm). A model of TU-1901 dual-beam ultraviolet-visible spectrophotometer (Beijing General Analysis General Instrument Co., Ltd., China) was scanned to obtain Abs spectra. HH-S2 electric heating constant temperature water bath (Jintan Dadi Automation Instrument Factory, China). UPW-N laboratory pure water system (Shanghai INESA Scientific Instrument Co., Ltd., China), KQ3200DB ultrasonic cleaner (Kunshan Ultrasonic Instrument Co., Ltd., China). KP-216 air energy light wave oven (Zhongshan Qiaokang Electric Manufacturing Co., Ltd., China), Electric heating constant temperature blast drying oven (DHG-9023A, Shanghai Jinghong Experimental Equipment Co., Ltd., China), 79-1 Magnetic heating stirrer (Jiangsu Zhongda Instrument Factory, China), High-speed refrigerated centrifuge (GL-25MS, Shanghai Lu Xiangyi Centrifuge Instrument Co., Ltd., China), pH meter (Mettler-Toledo Instruments Shanghai Co., Ltd., China) and SXZ-4-10TC ceramic fiber muffle furnace (Shanghai Shibei Instrument and Equipment Factory, China) were used.

Ferrocene (Fer, AR, Xilong Science Co., Ltd., China), 30 % H2O2 (Sichuan Xilong Science Co., Ltd., Chengdu, China), HCl (Sichuan Xilong Science Co., Ltd., Chengdu, China) and 0.1 mol/L Tris solution (Sichuan Xilong Science Co., Ltd., China) were used. A 0.1 mol/L Tris-HCl buffer solution (pH 4.4) was mixed 500 μL of 0.1 mol/L Tris and 500 μL of 2 mol/L HCl in a 10 mL water, and concentration was based on the amount of HCl. A 0.012 g 3,3′,5,5′-tetramethylbenzidine (TMB, storage at -8 ℃, T818493-5 g, CAS: 54827-17-7, Shanghai Macleans Biochemical Technology Co., Ltd., Shanghai, China) was dissolved in 100 mL of ethanol solution (ethanol: water = 1: 1) to obtain 0.5 mmol/L TMB. A 2.4 mmol/L ammonium molybdate (Sichuan Xilong Science Co., Ltd., Chengdu, China) was prepared as follows, 0.10 g ammonium molybdate was dissolved in 250 mL of 0.5 mol/L H2SO4.

Preparation of AgNPs: Firstly, 44 mL of ultrapure water was added to a conical flask on a magnetic stirrer. 2 mL of 10 mmol/L AgNO3, 2.0 mL of 100 mmol/L trisodium citrate, 600 μL of 30 % H2O2, and 600 μL of 0.1 mol/L NaBH4 were added dropwise and mixed well, then it was transferred into a convection oven immediately at 250 ℃ for 10 min to get orange-red transparent nano-silver glue. The nanosol was cooled naturally and diluted to 50 mL with ultrapure water to get AgNPs with a concentration of 4.0 × 10−4 mol/L. All reagents used were analytical grade, and the experimental water was ultrapure water.

Preparation of CDFe: 18 mg ferrocene powder was weighed accurately and added in 30 mL of ultrapure water. Then, it was placed in an ultrasonic cleaner and sonicated until the powder was evenly dispersed. The orange-yellow solution was transferred into an autoclave with polytetrafluoroethylene as the substrate. After sealing, it was reacted hydrothermally in a muffle furnace at an optimal temperature of 180 ℃ for 3 h. After the reaction was completed, it was cooled to room temperature with ice water to obtain a green and clear solution with a concentration of 0.6 mg/mL CDFe, which was diluted for later use.

In a 5.0 mL test tube, 75 μL of 6.0 × 10−3 mg/mL CDFe solution, 50 μL of 0.1 mol/L Tris-HCl solution, 125 μL of 0.5 mmol/L TMB solution and a certain amount of H2O2 solution of different concentrations were taken in turn, and the volume was fixed to 2.0 mL. After the solution was bathed in water at 50 ℃ for 30 min, the reaction was terminated by ice water, and then 400 μL of 0.4 mmol/L AgNPs was added into it. The SERS spectra were obtained by Raman spectrometer at light source power = 2.0 mW, slit = 50.0 μm. The SERS peak at the 1606 cm-1 was measured (I 1606), and ΔI 1606=I 1606-I 0 was calculated with no H2O2 added as a blank (I 0). For the Flu and Abs methods, no AgNPs were added.

In the system of CDFe-Tris-HCl-H2O2-TMB, CDFe could effectively catalyze H2O2 to form hydroxyl radicals (−OH), which oxidized TMB to TMBox molecular probe with good SERS activity. After silver nanoparticles were added to it, TMBox attached to the surface of them and produced a strong SERS peak at 1606 cm−1. With the increase of H2O2 concentration, SERS signals increased linearly. Based on this, a new SERS analysis method for simple determination of trace H2O2 was established (Fig. 1 ).

ce:section.ce:section-title: Electron microscopy and X-ray diffraction (XRD) of CDFe
ce:section.ce:para: Fig. 2 A shows that the CDFe is uniformly dispersed with a spherical shape. The particle size range of CDFe was found to be 1.2−5.2 nm and the average particle size was 2.6 nm. CDFe has an inter-particle distance of 2−5 nm, which may be the reason for the formation of more surface active ‘hot spots’, resulting in a high enhancement of SERS. The energy spectrum analysis of CDFe is provided in Fig. 2B. This spectrum shows a peak at 0.15 keV for C, a peak at 0.25 keV for O, two peaks at 1.0 and 6.4 keV for Fe, two peaks at 8.1 and 8.9 keV for Cu and a peak at 2.6 keV for Cl element. The results indicate that the product contained C, O and Fe elements ascribing to the precursor ferrocene. The peaks for Cu might come from the copper mesh and the peak for Cl might be originated from the impurity. The CDFe and ferrocene was further characterized by XRD analysis, as shown in Fig. 2C. The XRD pattern of the CDFe showed a diffraction peak centered at 26 °, which was consistent with the (002) lattice planes of graphitic carbon. Therefore, CDFe prepared by hydrothermal method with ferrocene as a precursor successfully achieved iron doping. In addition, the AgNPs as SERS substrate were spherical with size of 20 nm (Fig. S1) [46].

ce:section.ce:section-title: Abs/RRS/fluorescence/ Fourier transform infrared spectrometer (FTIR) spectra of CDFe
ce:section.ce:para: Fig. 3 A shows that Abs spectra with different concentrations of CDFe have a wide absorption band at 300−700 nm, and the absorbance at 620 nm increases gradually with the increase of CDFe concentration. Fig. 3B shows that the RRS intensity of CDFe (volt = 350 V, excited slit = emission slit = 5 nm) at 370 nm changes symbathically towards its concentration. Fig. 3C shows that the fluorescence peak of CDFe (volt = 500 V, excited slit = emission slit = 10 nm) is distributed roughly at 370 nm, and the fluorescence effect is strongest when the excitation wavelength (λEX) is 450 nm. Therefore, 450 nm was selected as the excitation wavelength. For Fig. 3C, the curve of 410 nm excited wavelength has the maximum fluorescence peak is not at 370 nm, but lower. This phenomenon is owing to the CDFe being of strong Rayleigh scattering, and the two peaks are too close. The fluorescence spectra of CDFe with different concentrations were obtained by scanning with fluorescence spectrophotometer. The result shows that the fluorescence intensity of CDFe at 460 nm is positively correlated with its concentration in the range of 6−600 μg/mL CDFe (Fig. 3D). Fig. 3E shows that CDFe has 6 characteristic peaks in the infrared spectroscopy (IR), 476.4 cm−1 is the characteristic peak of Fe-O, 815.3 cm−1 is caused by C–H out-of-plane bending vibration, 1000.5 cm−1 and 1105.6 cm−1 are caused by C–H in-plane deformation of substituted benzene, 1406.6 cm−1 is caused by CO stretching vibration, and 3461.7 cm−1 is due to the stretching vibration of COH or the crystal water in the test sample. Ferrocene has 5 peaks, of which 490.4 cm−1 is attributed to C–H stretching vibration, 778.8 cm−1 is attributed to C–H out-of-plane bending vibration, 1039.2 cm−1 is due to C–H in-plane bending vibration, 1139.9 cm−1 is due to the anti-stretching vibration of the ring, 1417.4 cm−1 is due to the CC stretching vibration, 1702.3 cm−1 is due to the CO stretching vibration of aldehydes, and 3374.9 cm−1 is due to COH stretching vibration or crystal water in the test sample. In summary, the structure of CDFe had changed during the hydrothermal process, formed Fe-O bonds, and introduced oxygen-containing groups to make it hydrophilic.

The SERS spectra with different CDFe concentrations were obtained by Raman spectrometer at light source power = 2.0 mW, slit = 50.0 μm. There are four strong SERS peaks at 1190, 1340, 1400 and 1606 cm−1 (Fig. 4 A) for the catalytic analytical system. The strongest peak at 1606 cm-1 was chosen for detection of H2O2. It shows that with the increase of H2O2 concentration, the amount of TMBox produced by catalysis increased, the SERS signal at 1606 cm-1 increases linearly. To further evaluate the SERS enhancement capability for varying the concentration of H2O2, we calculated relative SERS enhancement factor (EF) [47], EF = (ISERS/CSERS)/(IRS/CRS), where ISERS and IRS are the SERS and normal Raman intensities of the system, respectively. CSERS and CRS are the concentration of H2O2 corresponding to the above SERS and normal Raman intensities, respectively. The Raman and SERS spectra were recorded (Fig. S2), where IRS = 320 for CRS = 1.0 × 10−2 M H2O2, ISERS = 954 for the lowest detection concentration of 5.0 × 10−10 M H2O2, and ISERS = 7536 for the highest detection concentration of 2.5 × 10−8 M H2O2. Thus, both the EF is 5.96 × 107 and 9.42 × 108, respectively. Under the conditions of volt = 400 V, excited slit = emission slit = 5 nm, and excitation wavelength of 280 nm, the fluorescence spectra of the system were obtained by a fluorescence spectrophotometer, and the fluorescence peak intensity at 400 nm has a good linear relationship with its concentration (Fig. 4B). The Abs spectra of CDFe-Tris-HCl-H2O2-TMB system were obtained by scanning with ultraviolet-visible spectrophotometer. It shows that the system has two Abs peaks at 410 nm and 560 nm, and the latter is more sensitive and regular (Fig. 4C). With the increases of H2O2 concentration, the generated TMBox increased, and the color of the system solution gradually changed from colorless to purple, and the intensity of Abs peak at 560 nm also increased gradually. The results of Abs/fluorescence spectra were consistent with the results of SERS spectra, but the signal values were low and not as sensitive as the SERS method. However, the both methods were more simple than the SERS without AgNP substrate, the cost of Abs was lowest. In short, each of the three methods has its own advantages.

The experimental conditions affecting SERS analysis were studied according to the experimental method (2.3. Procedure). The results show that for CDFe-Tris-HCl-H2O2-TMB system, when the concentration of CDFe was 0.225 μg/mL, the concentration of TMB was 31 μmol/L, the concentration of pH 4.4 Tris-HCl was 2.5 mmol/L, the heating time of water bath was 30 min, and the temperature of water bath was 50 ℃, the system had the maximum ΔI 1606 value of SERS spectra. Therefore, the above parameters were selected as the optimal reaction conditions (Fig. S3).

In this paper, the SERS signal of CDFe-H2O2-TMB-Tris-HCl system was recorded. With the increase of H2O2 concentration, the amount of TMBox produced by catalysis increased, which made the SERS signal intensity increased. CH2O2 (nmol/L) and ΔI 1606 were plotted as working curve, ΔI 1606=I 1606-I 0 was calculated with no H2O2 added as a blank (I 0). The linear equation was ΔI 1606 = 311.7c+721.3 in the range of 5.0 × 10−10–2.5 × 10−8 mol/L H2O2, the coefficient was 0.9811, the detection limit was 2.52 × 10−10 mol/L. Compared with the reported H2O2 analysis methods [3–7,43], this SERS method has the advantages of high sensitivity and simple operation (Table S1).

According to the experimental method (2.3. Procedure), interference of coexisting ions on the determination of 10 nmol/L H2O2 by SERS was investigated. The SERS peak at the 1606 cm−1 was measured (I 1606), and ΔI 1606=I 1606-I 0 was calculated with no coexisting ions added as a blank (I 0). Then, relative error=ΔI 1606/I 0. The experimental results show that within the range of relative error not more than ±7 %, 1000 times of Na+, Zn2+, CO3 2, K+, I-, NH4 +, Ca2+, SiO3 2-, 500 times of Mn2+, Cu2+, Cr6+, SO4 2-, 200 times of Mg2+, Al3+, Br-, 100 times of Ba2+, Hg2+, Co2+, Fe3+, NO2- did not interfere with the H2O2 SERS measurement. Therefore, this method possessed favorable selectivity (Table S2).

In order to verify the reliability of this method, we selected three kinds of food samples including tremella fuciformis, kelp and black fungus that were taken from in a large supermarket. A 0.5 g tremella fuciformis, kelp and black fungus were accurately weighed with and added to 100 mL ultra-pure water, respectively. Then they were soaked for 60 min and centrifuged at 10,000 r/min for 3 min. The supernatants were filtered through 0.45 μm filter paper to obtain the sample solutions for use. Then, the H2O2 content was measured according to the experimental method (Table 1 ). The H2O2 content ranges were 0.20−0.25 μg/g for tremella, 0.53−0.59 μg/g for kelp and 0.32−0.39 μg/g for fungus. The relative standard deviations (RSD) were between 2.1 % and 4.3 %, and the recoveries were between 94.5 % and 103.8 %. The analytical results were agreement with those determined by the classical ammonium molybdate spectrophotometry (AMS) [48] (Table 1). There was no significant difference in the results of the two methods. In addition, this SERS method is more sensitive than the classical method, and can be used as good one of the determination methods of residual H2O2 in waterishlogged food products.

In this paper, the highly catalytic CDFe was prepared by hydrothermal method with ferrocene as precursor, which catalyzed H2O2 to generate −OH and oxidized TMB to generate TMBox molecular probe. Based on this, a new SERS method for the determination of trace H2O2 by catalytic amplification of CDFe was established. There was a good linear relationship between the concentration of H2O2 and the SERS signal under the selected conditions. The concentration range was 5.0 × 10−10–2.5 × 10−8 mol/L, and the detection limit was 2.52 × 10−10 mol/L. The method has been applied to the determination of H2O2 in waterishlogged food products, the recovery rate was 94.5 %–103.8 %, the precision was 2.1 %–4.3 %, and the results were satisfactory. In short, for the absorption method, it has the lowest cost, but the sensitivity is the lowest. For the SERS method, it has the highest sensitivity, but the equipment cost is highest. The sensitivity and cost of fluorescence method are between them. Compared with the single-mode method, there are more than two choices in the trimode method. In addition, the trimode method can perform with the one analytical system.

Jingjing Li: Conceptualization, Investigation, Data curation, Formal analysis, Writing – original draft. Yuxiang Zhao: Validation, Data curation, Writing – original draft. Lin Sun: Investigation, Software, Data curation. Aihui Liang: Writing – review & editing, Project administration, Supervision. Zhiliang Jiang: Conceptualization, Methodology, Writing – review & editing, Funding acquisition, Project administration, Supervision.

ce:conflict-of-interest.ce:para: The authors declare that they have no known competing financial interests or personal relationships that could have appeared to influence the work reported in this paper.

ce:acknowledgment.ce:para: This work was supported by the National Natural Science Foundation of China (No. 21767004).

ce:section.ce:label: Appendix A
ce:section.ce:para: Supplementary material related to this article can be found, in the online version, at doi:https://doi.org/10.1016/j.vibspec.2022.103334.

ce:section.ce:label: Appendix A
ce:section.ce:para: The following is Supplementary data to this article:
